skill.name: Red Team Tools and Methodology
skill.description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "follow red team methodology", "perform bug bounty hunting", "automate reconnaissance", "hunt for XSS vulnerabilities", "enumerate subdomains", or needs security researcher techniques and tool configurations from top bug bounty hunters.
skill.summary: Security researcher methodology for bug bounty reconnaissance, subdomain enumeration, and vulnerability discovery using industry-standard tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides documentation for legitimate security testing tools and methodologies used in authorized bug bounty programs. The detected 'external_commands' are standard reconnaissance tools (amass, subfinder, httpx, nuclei) documented for educational purposes. The 'network' findings are example URLs and public reconnaissance services. No malicious code, exploits, or unauthorized access methods are present. The skill appropriately includes constraints for authorized testing within scope.

medium_findings.title: External Command Documentation
medium_findings.description: Skill documents usage of security testing tools (amass, subfinder, httpx, nuclei). These are standard legitimate tools used in authorized bug bounty programs. No command injection vulnerability exists - these are documentation examples for running security tools.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documented command examples for running legitimate security tools. No user input is being passed to these commands - they are static examples for educational purposes.

medium_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance URLs
medium_findings.description: Contains example URLs and public reconnaissance services (BGP.he.net). These are standard resources used in authorized security testing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Uses example domain 'target.com' (reserved for documentation) and public BGP lookup service. Standard practice in security documentation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Methodology Documentation
low_findings.description: Skill documents reconnaissance techniques including subdomain enumeration and network scanning. This is standard bug bounty methodology and requires authorization via bug bounty program scope.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reconnaissance is a legitimate and expected part of authorized security testing. Skill includes constraints about respecting program scope.

content.value_statement: Security researchers need structured methodology to discover vulnerabilities efficiently. This skill provides proven workflows from top bug bounty hunters for subdomain enumeration, live host discovery, and vulnerability scanning.
actual_capabilities: Guide subdomain enumeration using Amass, Subfinder, and Assetfinder
Perform live host discovery with httpx and httprobe
Execute automated vulnerability scanning with Nuclei templates
Run content discovery and directory bruteforce with ffuf
Extract historical URLs using Wayback Machine and GAU
Build automated XSS testing pipelines with Dalfox
limitations: Does not include actual exploits or attack payloads
Requires user to have proper authorization for target systems
Tool installation and API keys are user responsibility
Does not perform testing - provides methodology and command references only

use_cases.title: Set Up Bug Bounty Recon Pipeline
use_cases.description: Automate the initial reconnaissance phase when starting a new bug bounty program engagement.
use_cases.target_user: Bug bounty hunters and security researchers

use_cases.title: Learn Professional Testing Workflows
use_cases.description: Study documented methodologies from experienced security professionals to improve testing efficiency.

use_cases.title: Reference Security Tool Commands
use_cases.description: Quickly look up correct syntax and options for common security testing tools during engagements.
use_cases.target_user: Red team members and penetration testers

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Subdomain Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to enumerate subdomains for target.com using passive reconnaissance tools.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new bug bounty target and need to find all subdomains

prompt_templates.title: Live Host Identification
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the workflow for discovering live web hosts from a list of subdomains?
prompt_templates.scenario: Have subdomain list and need to find which ones are actually responding

prompt_templates.title: Automated Vulnerability Scanning
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a comprehensive Nuclei scan workflow for discovered hosts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Want to run automated vulnerability scans on identified targets

prompt_templates.title: XSS Hunting Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an automated XSS testing pipeline using paramspider, Gxss, and dalfox.
prompt_templates.scenario: Need to efficiently test for XSS vulnerabilities across many parameters

output_examples.input: Show me subdomain enumeration commands for example.com
output_examples.output:
Here are the recommended subdomain enumeration commands:

1. Passive enumeration:
   amass enum -passive -d example.com -src
   subfinder -d example.com
   assetfinder --subs-only example.com

2. Active enumeration:
   amass enum -active -d example.com

3. Combine results:
   cat *.txt | sort -u > all_subs.txt

output_examples.input: How do I set up automated reconnaissance?
output_examples.output:
Here is a basic automated recon script structure:

1. Subdomain enumeration: subfinder -d $domain
2. Live host check: cat subs.txt | httpx
3. URL collection: cat live.txt | waybackurls
4. Vulnerability scan: nuclei -l live.txt

Each step feeds into the next for an automated pipeline.

best_practices: Always verify you have authorization before testing any target
Respect bug bounty program rules and scope limitations
Use rate limiting to avoid blocking or DoS
Manually verify automated tool findings before reporting
anti_patterns: Testing systems outside of authorized scope
Skipping manual verification of automated scanner results
Ignoring program rules about rate limits and testing restrictions

faq.question: Do I need special tools installed?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill documents commands for tools like Amass, Subfinder, httpx, Nuclei, and ffuf. You will need to install these tools on your system.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for penetration testing?
faq.answer: Yes, the methodology applies to both bug bounty and penetration testing engagements. Always ensure you have authorization.

faq.question: Does this skill run scans automatically?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides methodology and command references. You must execute the commands yourself with your own tools.

faq.question: Are API keys required?
faq.answer: Some advanced features require API keys for services like Shodan, Censys, or paid data sources. The skill works without them but with limited functionality.

faq.question: What is the target audience for this skill?
faq.answer: Bug bounty hunters, penetration testers, red team members, and security researchers who want structured testing methodologies.

faq.question: Is this skill legal to use?
faq.answer: The methodology is legal when used for authorized security testing. Always obtain proper authorization through bug bounty programs or penetration testing contracts.
